rationale: GT's one substantive non-blocking comment is that the docstring/module comment claiming 'original strict behaviour is preserved... other tests unaffected' is now false because the containment fallback applies unconditionally to all callers, not just opt-in escalation tests. Y hits this almost exactly, with a concrete failing example (hello vs. hello world goodbye) showing the practical consequence. X's closest finding reframes the same underlying fact as a request to run additional GLM TTS e2e tests rather than flagging the stale doc/contract, so it only tangentially overlaps. Neither candidate catches GT's minor (reviewer-flagged as skippable) note about check ordering/legacy producer liveness. X compensates with several additional well-grounded, highly actionable findings (character-level containment bug, dead code guard, missing priority-collision test) that GT didn't raise, giving it a slight actionability edge, but these don't improve recall against the actual GT concerns.
